root_cause.summary: The runner container's checked-out workspace at /work is owned by a different UID than the process that invokes git, so git refuses to operate with `fatal: detected dubious ownership in repository at '/work'`. The test harness falls back to running jest against an empty view of the repository, which reports `No tests found, exiting with code 1` for every example. The surface symptom is jest failing; the fix is upstream in the runner setup: add `git config --global --add safe.directory /work` (or equivalent) before running tests.

required_signals.value: fatal: detected dubious ownership in repository at '/work'
Git error: fatal: detected dubious ownership in repository at '/work'
No tests found, exiting with code 1

evidence_spans.reason: The root `fatal: detected dubious ownership` line plus the downstream warning that wraps it; this is the upstream cause that every later jest symptom traces back to.
Jest reports `No tests found` for multiple example projects and the run-tests.js harness emits ENOENT for missing results.json; these are symptoms of the root ownership issue.
GHA exit-code marker confirming the step failed.

must_not_claim: jest test assertion failed on a real test case
